confidence_rationale: Direct human-behavioural test of the consolidation arm of MECH-275: participants who slept after exposure to a statistically structured tone sequence showed enhanced extraction of the underlying regularities at re-test, while wake controls did not. Slow-wave sleep (SWS) duration positively correlated with the extraction effect, locating the mechanism in NREM sleep. Confidence moderate -- the statistical regularities in this task are surface-level transitional probabilities, not counterfactual-backed attribution structures, so the mapping to MECH-275 is to the regularity-extraction mechanism, not to the counterfactual-input requirement.
failure_signatures: The regularities in Durrant et al. are correlational (transitional probabilities between tones). MECH-275 specifies that aggregation over correlational input alone should produce noise-fit, not schema revision. Durrant et al. show extraction of correlational structure works; they do not test whether the same mechanism is what produces schema revision in REE's sense.

Effect size is moderate and the design tests recognition of structured vs random sequences -- it does not test whether the extracted structure is integrated with prior knowledge (the revision arm of MECH-275).

mapping.source_claim_statement: Adults exposed to a tone sequence governed by hidden transitional probabilities show post-sleep, but not post-wake, enhancement of their ability to discriminate structured from unstructured sequences. SWS duration correlates positively with the post-sleep gain. Sleep -- specifically NREM SWS -- consolidates statistical regularities that were only partially extracted during waking exposure.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-275 names a sleep-phase mechanism that aggregates across many waking traces and extracts the regularities common to them. Durrant et al. demonstrate this aggregation-and-extraction empirically: regularities that the waking system only partially extracted are stabilised and amplified by NREM sleep. The SWS-correlation localises the mechanism to the same sleep stage Lewis & Durrant 2011 names for schema construction.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The regularities here are correlational (transitional probabilities between tones), which is exactly the input MECH-275 says aggregation over alone produces noise-fit. The paper supports the regularity-extraction mechanism but does not bear on MECH-275's counterfactual-input requirement -- and arguably constitutes a partial counterexample, since the mechanism evidently also operates on correlational input. MECH-275 has to argue that the aggregated output of correlational input is shallow regularity, while the aggregated output of counterfactual-backed input is deeper schema revision.
mapping.source_context: Human behavioural study with EEG, n=16 per condition. Two-tone discrimination task with hidden Markov structure. Three groups: nap, wake, no-exposure. SWS positively correlated with structure-detection gain.

confidence_components.notes: Solid human behavioural study with electrophysiology. Mapping fidelity moderate because the input-type mismatch is real -- the paper supports the regularity-extraction mechanism but is silent on the counterfactual-vs-correlational distinction MECH-275 makes load-bearing. Transfer risk elevated because tone-sequence statistical learning to cross-domain Bayesian aggregation is a substantial extrapolation.
